sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient is a 54-year-old male who presents with a 2-3 month history of stabbing, shooting pain in the right cheekbone area of his face, which is exacerbated by cold temperatures and certain sensory stimuli.
He reports that the pain is severe, reaching a 10 out of 10 in intensity, and can last anywhere from a few minutes to an hour.
He denies numbness or tingling in the affected area and has not noticed any weakness on the right side of his face.
He has tried ibuprofen, Motrin, and tiger balm without relief.
His family doctor referred him to a neurosurgeon, but he has not undergone any further testing.
He reports occasional headaches, but no history of migraines.
Past medical history: The patient has no history of multiple sclerosis, brain tumors, or other neurological conditions.
Review of systems: The patient reports occasional headaches, but no other symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: The patient takes Tylenol for occasional headaches.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient had a normal smile and no weakness on the right side of his face.
He was able to follow his doctor's finger in both directions.
He reported a little bit of numbness on the right side of his face when his doctor ran a cotton tape along the side of his face.
The patient reported that a needle prick felt dull on the right side and sharp on the left side.
Test Results: The patient's MRI showed a small blood vessel abutting and pinching the trigeminal nerve.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a condition called trigeminal neuralgia, characterized by shooting electrical pain in the face due to compression of the trigeminal nerve by a small blood vessel.
Problem list: Trigeminal neuralgia; confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed Tegretol 100mg PO BID for the patient's trigeminal neuralgia.
I will have the patient follow up in one month to assess the efficacy of the medication.
We will also perform genetic testing to ensure the patient is a suitable candidate for the medication.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in one month to assess the efficacy of the Tegretol and discuss further treatment options if necessary.
